creator: brent@jfi.uchicago.edu
text:
All the same here as described above.  Freshly installed FreeBSD 10.1 with Samba 3.6.  LDAP lookups work fine from ldapsearch at the shell on the Samba machine, even using SSL port 636 (ldaps://).  LDAP server logs "TLS negotiation failure" when Samba starts and tries to connect.  smbd must be killed with '-2' or '-9' because it hangs and the rc script can't shut it down.

Similar to the previous report here, my LDAP servers are both running 8.4-STABLE.  Since they are running on jail servers which would have to be upgraded from the host systems on up to the jails, it would not be possible in the short term to upgrade the LDAP servers to 10.1.  (This problem also makes me worry of possible interoperability problems with older clients if I did have an upgraded server...don't know, haven't tried it.)  As is, the only problem I'm having connecting to existing LDAP servers is with FreeBSD 10,1 Samba client, so I'm keeping the LDAP servers on 8.4 for now.

I've been using pam_ldap and the nsswitch.conf like the other reports here, but just to see if it would work, I also tried setting up smb.conf to use the ldapsam backend so Samba would try to do LDAP on its own.  I setup the LDAP admin DN and password, and it still fails to establish an SSL connection.

So this seems to affect Samba whether you're doing SSL/TLS via PAM, or straight from Samba's own ldapsam backend.
